info.name: Google Workspace Admin SDK Directory API
info.description:
Manage users, groups, and organizational units in a Google Workspace domain. The Admin SDK Directory API lets administrators of enterprise domains view and manage resources such as user accounts, groups, and organizational units within their Google Workspace domain.

Contact Support:
 Name: Google Workspace Developer Support

item.name: Make a User a Super Administrator

variable.description: (Required) Identifies the user in the API request. The value can be the user primary email address, alias email address, or unique user ID.

request.description: Makes a user a super administrator. This can only be done by another super administrator.

response.name: Successfully updated admin status.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Authentication is required. The request did not include valid credentials.

response.name: Successfully undeleted user.

query.description: The domain name. Use this field to get groups from only one domain. To return all domains for a customer account, use the customer query parameter instead.
The unique ID for the customer Google Workspace account. In a multi-domain account, use this to list all groups for a customer. You can also use the my_customer alias.
Maximum number of results to return (1-500). Default is 100.
Token to specify the next page in the list.
Property to use for sorting results.
Whether to return results in ascending or descending order.
Query string for searching user fields. For more information on constructing user queries, see the Search for Users documentation.
If set to true, retrieves the list of deleted users.
What subset of fields to fetch for this user.
Whether to fetch the administrator-only or domain-wide public view of the user. Default is admin_view.
A comma-separated list of schema names. All fields from these schemas are fetched. Must be set when projection=custom.

request.description: Retrieves a paginated list of either deleted users or all users in a domain. Requires authorization with the admin.directory.user.readonly or admin.directory.user scope.

response.name: Successful response containing a list of users.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: The domain name. Use this field to get groups from only one domain. To return all domains for a customer account, use the customer query parameter instead.
The unique ID for the customer Google Workspace account. In a multi-domain account, use this to list all groups for a customer. You can also use the my_customer alias.
Maximum number of results to return (1-500). Default is 100.
Token to specify the next page in the list.
Property to use for sorting results.
Whether to return results in ascending or descending order.
Query string for searching user fields. For more information on constructing user queries, see the Search for Users documentation.
If set to true, retrieves the list of deleted users.
What subset of fields to fetch for this user.
Whether to fetch the administrator-only or domain-wide public view of the user. Default is admin_view.
A comma-separated list of schema names. All fields from these schemas are fetched. Must be set when projection=custom.

query.description: The domain name. Use this field to get groups from only one domain. To return all domains for a customer account, use the customer query parameter instead.
The unique ID for the customer Google Workspace account. In a multi-domain account, use this to list all groups for a customer. You can also use the my_customer alias.
Maximum number of results to return (1-500). Default is 100.
Token to specify the next page in the list.
Property to use for sorting results.
Whether to return results in ascending or descending order.
Query string for searching user fields. For more information on constructing user queries, see the Search for Users documentation.
If set to true, retrieves the list of deleted users.
What subset of fields to fetch for this user.
Whether to fetch the administrator-only or domain-wide public view of the user. Default is admin_view.
A comma-separated list of schema names. All fields from these schemas are fetched. Must be set when projection=custom.

query.description: The domain name. Use this field to get groups from only one domain. To return all domains for a customer account, use the customer query parameter instead.
The unique ID for the customer Google Workspace account. In a multi-domain account, use this to list all groups for a customer. You can also use the my_customer alias.
Maximum number of results to return (1-200). Default is 200.
Token to specify the next page in the list.
Column to use for sorting results.
Whether to return results in ascending or descending order.
Email or immutable ID of the user to list groups for. If specified, returns only those groups the user is a member of.
Query string for searching group fields. See the Searching for Groups documentation for supported fields.

request.description: Retrieves all groups of a domain or all groups a user is a member of. Requires authorization with the admin.directory.group.readonly or admin.directory.group scope.

response.name: Successful response containing a list of groups.
